mnemonics.title: SUSPEND Memory Trick
mnemonics.content: Think of SIGSUSPEND as: Signal-User Set-Unblock-Proceed-Enter-Next-Dead. It helps remember: 1) Signal 2) User sets 3) Unblock desired signals 4) Proceed to wait 5) Enter critical section 6) Next signal handler runs 7) Done - restore mask

acronyms.usage: Prefix for signal-related functions and constants in UNIX/POSIX systems

acronyms.expansion: Signal SUSPEND - temporarily SUSPEND process with custom signal MASK
acronyms.usage: Used when waiting for signals while controlling which signals can interrupt

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a gate that locks and closes atomically - you can't slip through when it's being set. sigsuspend() is like a one-way door that changes the lock settings and waits in one motion.
Think of the signal mask as a sieve/filters - blocked signals can't pass through, unblocked signals can. sigsuspend() temporarily changes which holes are open in the sieve.
Draw a timeline: Check flag → Signal arrives → call pause() → Signal missed → Sleep forever! sigsuspend() closes this gap by atomically changing mask AND waiting.

analogies.analogy: Like a bank vault with a combination lock that can only be set when the door is open - you can't change the combination while someone is trying to get in. sigsuspend() is one smooth motion.

Think of signal mask like a 'Do Not Disturb' mode on your phone - blocked signals are like notifications you won't see until you turn off DND. sigsuspend() lets you temporarily change DND settings to wait for one specific notification.
Like a security checkpoint that briefly pauses all traffic (signals) to let a VIP (specific signal) through, then returns to normal - that's sigsuspend() in action.
